item.title: [7] Quasiparticle dynamics in the 4d−4f Ising-like double perovskite Ba2DyRuO6 studied using neutron scattering and machine-learning framework
item.description:
Last authors: M. B. Stone, J. Sannigrahi, D. T. Adroja, and T. Basu

Authors: G. Roy, E. Kushwaha, M. Kumar, S. Ghosh, F. Orlandi, M. D. Le, M. B. Stone, J. Sannigrahi, D. T. Adroja, and T. Basu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study of magnetic excitations in correlated double perovskites, within user's broader interests.

Tags: #neutron-scattering #correlated-electrons

Abstract: Author(s): G. Roy, E. Kushwaha, M. Kumar, S. Ghosh, F. Orlandi, M. D. Le, M. B. Stone, J. Sannigrahi, D. T. Adroja, and T. Basu Double perovskites containing $4d\text{−}4f$ interactions provide a platform to study complex magnetic phenomena in correlated systems. Here, we investigate the magnetic ground state and quasiparticle excitations of the fascinating double perovskite system ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{2}{\mathrm{DyRuO}}_{6}$ thr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174416] Published Fri May 15, 2026

Basu Double perovskites containing $4d\text{−}4f$ interactions provide a platform to study complex magnetic phenomena in correlated systems. Here, we investigate the magnetic ground state and quasiparticle excitations of the fascinating double perovskite system ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{2}{\mathrm{DyRuO}}_{6}$ thr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174416] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [7] Topological hydrodynamics in ferromagnetic superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Bjørnulf Brekke, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak

Authors: Chau Dao, Eric Kleinherbers, Bjørnulf Brekke, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of triplet superconductors and topological order parameters; relevant to TSC interests.

Tags: #topological-superconductivity #ferromagnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Chau Dao, Eric Kleinherbers, Bjørnulf Brekke, and Yaroslav Tserkovnyak We explore the physical consequences of enriching the U(1) order parameter of conventional superconductors to a more topologically featureful space. As an illustrative case study, we focus on fully spin-polarized triplet superconductors, which are underlied by the SO(3) $\mathbf{d}$-vector order par… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174515] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Bjørnulf Brekke, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak

Authors: Chau Dao, Eric Kleinherbers, Bjørnulf Brekke, Yaroslav Tserkovnyak

Abstract: Author(s): Chau Dao, Eric Kleinherbers, Bjørnulf Brekke, and Yaroslav Tserkovnyak We explore the physical consequences of enriching the U(1) order parameter of conventional superconductors to a more topologically featureful space. As an illustrative case study, we focus on fully spin-polarized triplet superconductors, which are underlied by the SO(3) $\mathbf{d}$-vector order par… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174515] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [8] Large spontaneous Hall effect arising from collinear antiferromagnetism in Ce2PtGe6
item.description:
Last authors: Keiki Takeda, Hisashi Kotegawa

Authors: Hayata Matsuda, Ruo Hibino, Chihiro Tabata, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental observation of anomalous Hall effect in an antiferromagnet; high relevance to magnetic topological materials.

Tags: #anomalous-Hall #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Hayata Matsuda, Ruo Hibino, Chihiro Tabata, Koji Kaneko, Nonoka Higa, Takahiro Onimaru, Hiroto Tanaka, Hideki Tou, Hitoshi Sugawara, Junichi Hayashi, Keiki Takeda, and Hisashi Kotegawa The spontaneous Hall effect, corresponding to a zero-field anomalous Hall effect (AHE), is induced by symmetry breaking associated with ferromagnetism. Studies in recent years, however, have revealed that antiferromagnetic (AFM) states characterized by magnetic point groups that allow ferromagnetism… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195116] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Keiki Takeda, Hisashi Kotegawa

Authors: Hayata Matsuda, Ruo Hibino, Chihiro Tabata, et al.

Studies in recent years, however, have revealed that antiferromagnetic (AFM) states characterized by magnetic point groups that allow ferromagnetism… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195116] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [10] Interlayer hybridization and emergent near-Fermi-level three-dimensional dispersions in intercalated transition metal dichalcogenides
item.description:
Last authors: Zhong-Yi Lu, Zhonghao Liu

Authors: Longli Tian, Hongrun Zhen, Pengcheng Ma, et al.

Score 10/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: Direct hit: ARPES study of electronic structure and hybridization in intercalated TMDs.

Tags: #ARPES #TMD #electronic-structure

Abstract: Author(s): Longli Tian, Hongrun Zhen, Pengcheng Ma, Tianqi Wang, Yin Yang, Huancheng Yang, Fanyu Meng, Hechang Lei, Zhong-Yi Lu, and Zhonghao Liu Intercalation of $3d$ atoms into layered transition-metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) provides an effective route to tune their magnetism and electronic structure. Using photon-energy-dependent angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, we show that Cr intercalation in $2H\text{−}{\mathrm{NbS}}_{2}$ intr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195119] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Zhong-Yi Lu, Zhonghao Liu

Authors: Longli Tian, Hongrun Zhen, Pengcheng Ma, et al.

item.title: [9] Enhanced anomalous Hall response via nodal-line tuning in Co2VSn1−xAlx topological semimetal
item.description:
Last authors: Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, Sanjay Singh

Authors: Shivani Rastogi, Gaurav K. Shukla, Nidhi, Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, Sanjay Singh

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: Topological semimetal with Berry-curvature driven anomalous Hall effect; very high relevance.

Tags: #topological-semimetal #anomalous-Hall #Berry-curvature

Abstract: Author(s): Shivani Rastogi, Gaurav K. Shukla, Nidhi, Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, and Sanjay Singh Topological semimetals (TSMs), known for their topologically protected band crossings, give rise to a range of intriguing properties, including the anomalous Hall effect governed by Berry curvature in magnetic TSMs. Among these, cobalt-based magnetic TSMs, in particular, have been reported to exhibi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195121] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, Sanjay Singh

Authors: Shivani Rastogi, Gaurav K. Shukla, Nidhi, Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, Sanjay Singh

Why: Topological semimetal with Berry-curvature driven anomalous Hall effect; very high relevance.

Abstract: Author(s): Shivani Rastogi, Gaurav K. Shukla, Nidhi, Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, and Sanjay Singh Topological semimetals (TSMs), known for their topologically protected band crossings, give rise to a range of intriguing properties, including the anomalous Hall effect governed by Berry curvature in magnetic TSMs. Among these, cobalt-based magnetic TSMs, in particular, have been reported to exhibi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195121] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [8] Polarization dependency in resonant inelastic x-ray scattering
item.description:
Last authors: Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

Authors: Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Important RIXS methodology paper for interpreting scattering geometry and polarization dependencies.

Tags: #RIXS #spectroscopy

Abstract: Author(s): Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, and Maurits W. Haverkort Resonant Inelastic x-ray Scattering (RIXS) is a well-established tool for probing excitations in a wide range of materials. The measured spectra strongly depend on the scattering geometry via its influence on the polarization of the incoming and outgoing light. By employing a tensor representation o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195122] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

Authors: Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

Haverkort Resonant Inelastic x-ray Scattering (RIXS) is a well-established tool for probing excitations in a wide range of materials. The measured spectra strongly depend on the scattering geometry via its influence on the polarization of the incoming and outgoing light. By employing a tensor representation o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195122] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [7] Asymmetric scattering drives large nonlinear Nernst and Seebeck effects
item.description:
Last authors: Harsh Varshney, Amit Agarwal

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of Berry-curvature related transport in trilayer graphene; explains experimental signals.

Tags: #graphene #Berry-curvature #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Harsh Varshney and Amit Agarwal The authors show here that disorder can be a dominant source of nonlinear thermoelectricity. They develop a unified semiclassical theory of nonlinear Nernst and Seebeck effects that incorporates side-jump and skew-scattering processes, identifying six distinct second-order contributions with a clear band-geometric origin. For A B A -stacked trilayer graphene, the theory explains the large experimentally observed signals and provides symmetry-based diagnostics to distinguish intrinsic and extrinsic mechanisms. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195417] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Harsh Varshney, Amit Agarwal

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of Berry-curvature related transport in trilayer graphene; explains experimental signals.

Tags: #graphene #Berry-curvature #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Harsh Varshney and Amit Agarwal The authors show here that disorder can be a dominant source of nonlinear thermoelectricity. They develop a unified semiclassical theory of nonlinear Nernst and Seebeck effects that incorporates side-jump and skew-scattering processes, identifying six distinct second-order contributions with a clear band-geometric origin. For A B A -stacked trilayer graphene, the theory explains the large experimentally observed signals and provides symmetry-based diagnostics to distinguish intrinsic and extrinsic mechanisms. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195417] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Asymmetric scattering drives large nonlinear Nernst and Seebeck effects
item.dc:creator: Harsh Varshney, Amit Agarwal

item.title: [8] Observation of one-dimensional edge states between even and odd quintuple layers of ultrathin Bi2Te3
item.description:
Last authors: Yanzhao Liu, Jian Wang

Authors: Wei Ren, Zhen Liang, Tianheng Wei, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental observation of topological edge states in a core topological insulator material.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #Bi2Te3

Abstract: Author(s): Wei Ren, Zhen Liang, Tianheng Wei, Kun Dong, Zhuoran Xu, Shichao Qi, Feitong Song, Ji Feng, Yanzhao Liu, and Jian Wang Topological insulators represent a class of quantum materials exhibiting a host of unique physical properties. As an important category of topological insulators, quantum spin Hall insulators show one-dimensional topological helical edge states. Here, we report the successful growth of the high-qual… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195420] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [7] Current switching behavior mediated via hinge modes in higher-order topological phases using altermagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Authors: Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretically connects altermagnetism with higher-order topological insulators, both areas of interest.

Tags: #altermagnetism #topologicalinsulator

Abstract: Author(s): Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, and Arijit Saha We propose a theoretical framework to engineer hybrid-order and higher-order topological phases in three-dimensional topological insulators by coupling to $d$-wave altermagnets (AMs). The presence of only a ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-type AM drives the system into a hybrid-order topological phase where… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195421] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Authors: Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretically connects altermagnetism with higher-order topological insulators, both areas of interest.

Tags: #altermagnetism #topologicalinsulator

Abstract: Author(s): Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, and Arijit Saha We propose a theoretical framework to engineer hybrid-order and higher-order topological phases in three-dimensional topological insulators by coupling to $d$-wave altermagnets (AMs). The presence of only a ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-type AM drives the system into a hybrid-order topological phase where… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195421] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [7] Quantum geometric map of magnetotransport
item.description:
Last authors: Fuming Xu, Jian Wang

Authors: Longjun Xiang, Jinxiong Jia, Fuming Xu, Jian Wang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical framework connecting Berry curvature and quantum metric to experimental Hall effects.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #Halleffect

Abstract: Author(s): Longjun Xiang, Jinxiong Jia, Fuming Xu, and Jian Wang Here, the authors establish a unified quantum geometric map for magnetotransport, revealing that magnetononlinear Hall, planar Hall, and ordinary Hall effects originate from distinct quantum geometric quantities, including Zeeman quantum metric and Berry curvature multipoles. This framework uncovers previously overlooked interband contributions to the ordinary Hall effect and predicts a step-like spin-induced planar Hall response, providing a powerful guide for interpreting and designing magnetotransport experiments in quantum materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201406] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Fuming Xu, Jian Wang

Authors: Longjun Xiang, Jinxiong Jia, Fuming Xu, Jian Wang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical framework connecting Berry curvature and quantum metric to experimental Hall effects.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #Halleffect

Abstract: Author(s): Longjun Xiang, Jinxiong Jia, Fuming Xu, and Jian Wang Here, the authors establish a unified quantum geometric map for magnetotransport, revealing that magnetononlinear Hall, planar Hall, and ordinary Hall effects originate from distinct quantum geometric quantities, including Zeeman quantum metric and Berry curvature multipoles. This framework uncovers previously overlooked interband contributions to the ordinary Hall effect and predicts a step-like spin-induced planar Hall response, providing a powerful guide for interpreting and designing magnetotransport experiments in quantum materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201406] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quantum geometric map of magnetotransport

item.title: [7] Spin-liquid-like spin dynamics in the frustrated antiferromagnet TbBO3
item.description:
Last authors: D. T. Adroja, P. Khuntia

Authors: J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of frustrated magnetism and spin-liquid behavior in a rare-earth system.

Tags: #quantummagnetism #spinliquid

Abstract: Author(s): J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, M. Pregelj, K. Kargeti, S. K. Panda, G. B. G. Stenning, P. Manuel, M. D. Le, D. T. Adroja, and P. Khuntia The synergistic interplay between spin correlations, spin-orbit coupling, and competing exchange interactions provides a promising route to realize exotic quantum states with nontrivial excitations in rare-earth-based frustrated magnets. Here, by using thermodynamic and local-probe measurements down… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174406] Published Thu May 14, 2026

Last authors: D. T. Adroja, P. Khuntia

Authors: J. Khatua, D. Tay, T. Shiroka, et al.

Khuntia The synergistic interplay between spin correlations, spin-orbit coupling, and competing exchange interactions provides a promising route to realize exotic quantum states with nontrivial excitations in rare-earth-based frustrated magnets. Here, by using thermodynamic and local-probe measurements down… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174406] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: [9] 3Q magnetic order with spatially alternating spin scalar chirality in overdoped Co0.336TaS2
item.description:
Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park

Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant multi-Q spin textures in a dichalcogenide system with topological Hall implications.

Tags: #TaS2 #neutronscattering #topologicalHall

Abstract: Author(s): Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, Yeochan An, Kazuki Iida, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Sakib Matin, Romain Sibille, Scott A. Crooker, and Je-Geun Park While underdoped Co 1 / 3 TaS 2 shows a topological Hall effect, the overdoped Co 0 . 336 TaS 2 variant does not. Traditionally, this was attributed to a simple helical spin order. However, using multimodal techniques—including inelastic neutron scattering and optical dichroism—the authors propose here a more complex triple-Q order. In this state, the spin scalar chirality alternates spatially, effectively cancelling the macroscopic Hall response. This study provides a new framework for detecting “hidden” multi-Q spin textures that standard transport measurements might miss. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174410] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park

Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant multi-Q spin textures in a dichalcogenide system with topological Hall implications.

Tags: #TaS2 #neutronscattering #topologicalHall

Abstract: Author(s): Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, Yeochan An, Kazuki Iida, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Sakib Matin, Romain Sibille, Scott A. Crooker, and Je-Geun Park While underdoped Co 1 / 3 TaS 2 shows a topological Hall effect, the overdoped Co 0 . 336 TaS 2 variant does not. Traditionally, this was attributed to a simple helical spin order. However, using multimodal techniques—including inelastic neutron scattering and optical dichroism—the authors propose here a more complex triple-Q order. In this state, the spin scalar chirality alternates spatially, effectively cancelling the macroscopic Hall response. This study provides a new framework for detecting “hidden” multi-Q spin textures that standard transport measurements might miss. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174410] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] 3Q magnetic order with spatially alternating spin scalar chirality in overdoped Co0.336TaS2
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park; Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.

item.title: [7] Multidimensional control of altermagnetism via symmetry engineering in van der Waals heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Ke-Qiu Chen, Li-Ming Tang

Authors: Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigates altermagnetism in vdW heterostructures, matching the user's magnetism and vdW interests.

Tags: #altermagnetism #vdWheterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, Shibo Fang, Yee Sin Ang, Xiong-Xiong Xue, Ke-Qiu Chen, and Li-Ming Tang Altermagnets enable nonrelativistic spin splitting without net magnetization, benefiting miniaturized and highly integrated spintronic devices. In this work, we investigate the physical mechanism by which ferroelectric substrates induce altermagnetism in antiferromagnetic materials through symmetry … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174411] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Ke-Qiu Chen, Li-Ming Tang

Authors: Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigates altermagnetism in vdW heterostructures, matching the user's magnetism and vdW interests.

Tags: #altermagnetism #vdWheterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, Shibo Fang, Yee Sin Ang, Xiong-Xiong Xue, Ke-Qiu Chen, and Li-Ming Tang Altermagnets enable nonrelativistic spin splitting without net magnetization, benefiting miniaturized and highly integrated spintronic devices. In this work, we investigate the physical mechanism by which ferroelectric substrates induce altermagnetism in antiferromagnetic materials through symmetry … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174411] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Multidimensional control of altermagnetism via symmetry engineering in van der Waals heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Ke-Qiu Chen, Li-Ming Tang; Authors: Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, et al.

item.title: [8] Dependence of superconducting and pseudogap quasiparticle dynamics on the number of CuO2 planes in Bi-based cuprate superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Hiroshi Eisaki, Migaku Oda

Authors: Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of quasiparticle dynamics in cuprates, a key unconventional superconductor class.

Tags: #cuprates #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, Yasunori Toda, Shigeyuki Ishida, Hiroshi Eisaki, and Migaku Oda The maximum superconducting transition temperature ${T}_{c,\mathrm{max}}$ in cuprate superconductors exhibits a strong dependence on the number of ${\mathrm{CuO}}_{2}$ planes $n$ per structural cell, exceeding 100 K in trilayer compounds ($n=3$). Elucidating the mechanism responsible for this enhanc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174510] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [7] Theory of reentrant superconductivity in Corbino Josephson junctions
item.description:
Last authors: Philip Kim, Yuval Oreg

Authors: Omri Lesser, Joon Young Park, Yuval Ronen, Thomas Werkmeister, Philip Kim, Yuval Oreg

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant to topological insulator-superconductor junctions and their transport signatures.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Omri Lesser, Joon Young Park, Yuval Ronen, Thomas Werkmeister, Philip Kim, and Yuval Oreg Josephson junctions made of conventional superconductors display Fraunhofer-like oscillations of the critical current as a function of the threaded magnetic flux. When the superconductors are deposited on the surface of a three-dimensional topological insulator, this pattern is slightly modified due… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174511] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [8] Inherent momentum-dependent gap structure of altermagnetic superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Andreas Kreisel, Brian M. Andersen

Authors: Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Predictions for momentum-dependent superconducting gaps in altermagnets, directly relevant to ARPES.

Tags: #altermagnetism #superconductivity #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, Mercè Roig, Daniel F. Agterberg, Andreas Kreisel, and Brian M. Andersen The authors study here superconductivity in altermagnetic metals using microscopic models, investigating the importance of the sublattice degree of freedom. They demonstrate that the sublattice polarization of the altermagnetic state imprints an anisotropic structure on the superconducting gap with nodes at the Brillouin zone boundary, despite arising from a momentum-independent bare interaction. In the limit of large spin-split bands, nonunitary equal-spin triplet superconductivity is favored by longer-range same-sublattice pairing. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174513] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Andreas Kreisel, Brian M. Andersen

Authors: Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Predictions for momentum-dependent superconducting gaps in altermagnets, directly relevant to ARPES.

Tags: #altermagnetism #superconductivity #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, Mercè Roig, Daniel F. Agterberg, Andreas Kreisel, and Brian M. Andersen The authors study here superconductivity in altermagnetic metals using microscopic models, investigating the importance of the sublattice degree of freedom. They demonstrate that the sublattice polarization of the altermagnetic state imprints an anisotropic structure on the superconducting gap with nodes at the Brillouin zone boundary, despite arising from a momentum-independent bare interaction. In the limit of large spin-split bands, nonunitary equal-spin triplet superconductivity is favored by longer-range same-sublattice pairing. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174513] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Inherent momentum-dependent gap structure of altermagnetic superconductors
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Andreas Kreisel, Brian M. Andersen; Authors: Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, et al.

item.title: [7] Strange metal and Fermi arcs from disordering spin stripes
item.description:
Last authors: Xu Zhang, Nick Bultinck

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory exploring nematicity and Fermi arcs, directly relevant to electronic structure interests.

Tags: #nematicity #Fermiarcs #strangemetal

Abstract: Author(s): Xu Zhang and Nick Bultinck We revisit the effective theory for fluctuating spin stripes coupled to a Fermi surface and consider the parameter regime where a spin-nematic phase intervenes between the spin-density-wave state and the symmetric state. It is shown that adding potential disorder to this theory, which acts as an unc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195112] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Xu Zhang, Nick Bultinck

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Tags: #nematicity #Fermiarcs #strangemetal

It is shown that adding potential disorder to this theory, which acts as an unc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195112] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [8] Pressure-induced electronic band evolution and observation of superconductivity in the Dirac semimetal ZrTe5

Tags: #ZrTe5 #Diracsemimetal #topological #bandevolution

Abstract: Author(s): Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, Rajan Walia, Jianping Sun, Genfu Chen, Dilip Bhoi, Sandip Chatterjee, Yoshiya Uwatoko, Jinguang Cheng, and Prashant Shahi We report a comprehensive investigation of the pressure effects on the magnetotransport properties of the topological material ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{5}$ within 1–8 GPa pressure range. With increasing pressure, the characteristic peak (${T}_{p}$) in its electrical resistivity $ρ(T)$ first shifts to highe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195113] Published Thu May 14, 2026

Gangwar, Rajan Walia, Jianping Sun, Genfu Chen, Dilip Bhoi, Sandip Chatterjee, Yoshiya Uwatoko, Jinguang Cheng, and Prashant Shahi We report a comprehensive investigation of the pressure effects on the magnetotransport properties of the topological material ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{5}$ within 1–8 GPa pressure range. With increasing pressure, the characteristic peak (${T}_{p}$) in its electrical resistivity $ρ(T)$ first shifts to highe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195113] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [7] Interfacial bond tailored altermagnetic tunnel junctions
item.description:
Last authors: Jinbo Yang, Jie Yang

Authors: Chao Mao, Shiqi Liu, Shunfang Li, Jinbo Yang, Jie Yang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Device design based on altermagnetism, a material class of significant interest.

Tags: #altermagnetism #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Chao Mao, Shiqi Liu, Shunfang Li, Jinbo Yang, and Jie Yang Altermagnets, a distinctive class of unconventional antiferromagnets featuring compensated magnetization but ferromagnetlike spin splitting, have attracted significant interest as promising candidates for next-generation memories. Here, we design an altermagnetic tunnel junction (AMTJ) based on the … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174409] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: [9] Doping evolution of the charge density wave and charge density fluctuations in La2−xSrxCuO4

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental CDW and charge fluctuation study in a major unconventional superconductor class.

Tags: #CDW #cuprate #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Charles C. Tam, Mengze Zhu, Maud C. Barthélemy, Lauren J. Cane, Oliver J. Lipscombe, Stefano Agrestini, Jaewon Choi, Mirian Garcia-Fernandez, Ke-Jin Zhou, and Stephen M. Hayden Cuprate superconductors show various collective charge correlations that are intimately connected with their electronic properties. In particular, charge order in the form of an incommensurate charge density wave (CDW) order with an in-plane wave vector ${δ}_{\text{CDW}}≈$ 0.23–0.35 reciprocal latti… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174506] Published Wed May 13, 2026

Rev. B 113, 195109] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: [8] Phonons drive the topological phase transition in quasi-one-dimensional Bi4I4
item.description:
Last authors: Jin-Jian Zhou, Yugui Yao

Authors: Wenjie Hu, Jiayi Gong, Yuhui Qiu, Lexian Yang, Jin-Jian Zhou, Yugui Yao

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Electronic structure and topological phase transition in a quasi-1D material.

Tags: #quasi-1D #topological #phonon

Abstract: Author(s): Wenjie Hu, Jiayi Gong, Yuhui Qiu, Lexian Yang, Jin-Jian Zhou, and Yugui Yao Quasi-one-dimensional bismuth halides offer an exceptional platform for exploring diverse topological phases, yet the nature of the room-temperature topological phase transition in ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{I}}_{4}$ remains unresolved. While theory predicts the high-temperature $β$ phase to be a st… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201111] Published Wed May 13, 2026

While theory predicts the high-temperature $β$ phase to be a st… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201111] Published Wed May 13, 2026
